prompt:
Testing our retry/heartbeat logic against real setTimeout has made the CI suite both slow AND flaky, so I want a deterministic scheduler we fully control. scheduler.ts, export class VirtualScheduler. No real timers anywhere in it — time only moves when the caller says so.

API + semantics (test_scheduler.ts is the source of truth, `node --test test_scheduler.ts`):
- now(): current virtual ms, starts at 0
- schedule(fn, delayMs): one-shot, returns an id. delayMs < 0 is a RangeError; 0 is fine and fires on the next advance, even advance(0)
- scheduleRepeating(fn, intervalMs): fires at every exact multiple of intervalMs from when it was scheduled — drift-free, meaning one advance(35) with interval 10 fires at t=10, 20, 30 (three times), and the NEXT fire is still t=40, no matter how ragged the advances were. intervalMs <= 0 is a RangeError
- cancel(id): true if it cancelled something pending, false otherwise; works for both kinds; cancelling from inside a callback must stop a not-yet-run task even at the same timestamp
- advance(ms): runs everything due in (now, now+ms], strictly in fire-time order, FIFO for ties; inside a callback now() reports that callback's scheduled fire time, not the advance target; tasks a callback schedules during the advance also run if they land inside the window; afterwards now() is the advance target
- advanceTo(t): absolute-time sugar for the same thing
- pending(): count of live scheduled tasks (a repeater counts once)

One more thing that bit us with our homegrown attempt: if a callback throws, let the exception surface out of advance() — but don't corrupt the scheduler. now() should sit at the failed task's fire time and everything still queued must run on a later advance.

Node's built-in test runner, zero deps, don't edit the tests.
